zh.lifeSummary: 當Isabel de Gras於1314年出生在英國坎布利其爾維波爾時，她的父親Sir John Wimpole de Gras29歲，母親Paulina Grace de Allington24歲。她和Sir Thomas of Ripon De Bourne至少有2個兒子和2個女兒。她去世於1363年在英國英格蘭越可爾享年49歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Gras
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Clemence, Francoise, Michel, Philippe.  Spanish Francisco, Baudilio, Guadalupe, Jesus, Jose Pedro, Juan, Juana.  German Dieter.
French and Catalan: nickname for a stout person, from gras ‘fat’ (from Latin crassus, Old French cras). Compare Degrace .
French: habitational name from (Le) Gras, the name of several places in various parts of France.

variants.name: Degrace
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Emile, Monique.
French Canadian (Degrâce): altered form of Catalan Gras ‘fat’. Compare Degrasse .
French (Degrâce): habitational name for someone from La Grâce in Marne, named from La Grâce-Notre-Dame, an abbey founded in the 13th century near Montmirail.

variants.name: Graeser
meanings: German (also Gräser): variant of the topographic name Gras , the -er suffix denoting an inhabitant.

nameMeanings.name: Isabel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabell
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isabelle
meanings: form of   Isabel  , now in frequent use in the English-speaking world.

variants.name: Isobella
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

variants.name: Isobelle
meanings: Originally a Spanish version of   Elizabeth  , which was coined by deletion of the first syllable and alteration of the final consonant sound to one that can normally end a word in Spanish. The name was imported into France in the early Middle Ages, and thence into England. It was a royal name, and its enduring popularity may have been enhanced by the fact that it was borne by a queen of England—  Isabella  (  1296–1358 ), daughter of  Philip IV  of France—even though she led a turbulent life and eventually had her husband,  Edward II , murdered.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

historicEvents.title: The Hundred Years' War
historicEvents.description: The Hundred Years' War between France and England was largely due to the question of legitimate succession to the French crown.

en.lifeSummary: When Isabel de Gras was born in 1314, in Wimpole, Cambridgeshire, England, her father, Sir John Wimpole de Gras, was 29 and her mother, Paulina Grace de Allington, was 24. She had at least 2 sons and 2 daughters with Sir Thomas of Ripon De Bourne. She died in 1363, in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 49.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Isabel de Gras est née en 1314, à Wimpole, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir John Wimpole de Gras, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Paulina Grace de Allington, avait 24 ans. Elle a eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles avec Sir Thomas of Ripon De Bourne. Elle est décédée en 1363, à Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 49 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Isabel de Gras 1314 in Wimpole, Cambridgeshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir John Wimpole de Gras 29 und ihre Mutter Paulina Grace de Allington 24. Sie hatte mit Sir Thomas of Ripon De Bourne mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1363 im Alter von 49 Jahren in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel de Gras è nata il nel 1314 nel Wimpole, Cambridgeshire, England, sua padre, Sir John Wimpole de Gras, aveva 29 anni e sua madre, Paulina Grace de Allington, aveva 24 anni. Lei avuto almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie con Sir Thomas of Ripon De Bourne. Morì nel 1363, nel Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 49 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Isabel de Grasが1314年にイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアウィンプオルで生まれた時，父Sir John Wimpole de Grasは29歳，母Paulina Grace de Allingtonは24歳であった。彼女はSir Thomas of Ripon De Bourneとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1363年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアで49歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Isabel de Gras(이)가 1314년 영국 캄브리지실르 위포일에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir John Wimpole de Gras(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Paulina Grace de Allington(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 Sir Thomas of Ripon De Bourne(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1363년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르에서 49세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Isabel de Gras nasceu em 1314, em Wimpole, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir John Wimpole de Gras, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Paulina Grace de Allington, tinha 24 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas com Sir Thomas of Ripon De Bourne. Ela faleceu em 1363, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 49 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Isabel de Gras родилась в 1314 г. в местности вимпол, камбриджешир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir John Wimpole de Gras было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Paulina Grace de Allington, 24. У неё было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери от Sir Thomas of Ripon De Bourne. Она умерла в 1363 г. в местности еркшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 49 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Isabel de Gras nació en 1314, en Wimpole, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir John Wimpole de Gras, tenía 29 años y su mamá, Paulina Grace de Allington, tenía 24 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas con Sir Thomas of Ripon De Bourne. Murió en 1363, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 49 años.
